title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated environment and install required packages for CROSS-GRN experiments

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.1+ with CUDA 12.1: pip install torch==2.1.0 torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install single-cell analysis packages: pip install scanpy==1.9.6 anndata==0.10.0 scvi-tools==1.0.4
steps.step4: Install additional dependencies: pip install numpy pandas scikit-learn scipy matplotlib seaborn pyarrow
steps.step5: Install evaluation packages: pip install torchmetrics==1.2.0 wandb
steps.step6: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step7: Create output directories: mkdir -p data models results figures logs

title: Download and Preprocess 10x Genomics PBMC Multiome
description: Download the primary benchmark dataset (10k cells) and preprocess for training

steps.step1: Download 10x PBMC Multiome from 10x website: wget https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-arc/2.0.0/pbmc_granulocyte_sorted_10k/pbmc_granulocyte_sorted_10k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5
steps.step2: Load data with scanpy: adata = sc.read_10x_h5('pbmc_granulocyte_sorted_10k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5', gex_only=False)
steps.step3: Split RNA and ATAC: rna = adata[:, adata.var.feature_types == 'Gene Expression'].copy(); atac = adata[:, adata.var.feature_types == 'Peaks'].copy()
steps.step4: Filter genes: sc.pp.filter_genes(rna, min_cells=50) - keep ~5,000 highly variable genes
steps.step5: Filter peaks: sc.pp.filter_genes(atac, min_cells=20) - keep ~20,000 accessible peaks
steps.step7: Normalize ATAC: TF-IDF normalization using atac.tl.tfidf()
steps.step9: Cell type annotation: Use marker genes to annotate major cell types (T cells, B cells, Monocytes, NK cells, DCs) - ~10 types
steps.step10: Create peak-gene mapping: Map peaks to genes within +/- 500kb using genomic coordinates
steps.step11: Get TF list: Load list of ~1,500 human TFs from Lambert et al. 2018

title: Download SHARE-seq Mouse Skin Dataset
description: Download secondary dataset for generalization testing (~15k cells)

steps.step1: Download SHARE-seq data from GEO GSE140203: Use wget to download processed h5ad
steps.step2: Load data: adata = sc.read_h5ad('shareseq_mouse_skin.h5ad')
steps.step3: Apply same preprocessing pipeline as PBMC: filter genes/peaks, normalize, select HVGs
steps.step4: Cell type annotation: Use provided cluster labels or marker-based annotation
steps.step5: Save preprocessed: data/shareseq_preprocessed.h5ad

title: Prepare Ground Truth and Evaluation Data
description: Load ChIP-seq validation data and TRRUST database for evaluation

steps.step1: Download TRRUST v2 database: wget https://www.grnpedia.org/trrust/data/trrust_rawdata.human.tsv
steps.step2: Parse TRRUST: Create dictionary of TF -> target edges with sign (activation/repression)
steps.step3: Download ChIP-seq gold standard: Use ENCODE ChIP-seq peaks for key TFs (SPI1, CEBPB, etc.)
steps.step4: Process ChIP-seq: Create binary labels for TF-target pairs based on peak presence in promoter
steps.step5: Create train/val/test splits: 70/15/15 split stratified by cell type, use random_state=42
steps.step6: Save evaluation data: data/trrust_network.json, data/chipseq_labels.json, data/splits.json

title: Implement and Run GENIE3 + Correlation Baseline
description: Simple baseline using tree-based feature importance with correlation sign

steps.step1: Implement GENIE3: Use sklearn RandomForestRegressor to predict each gene from TFs
steps.step2: Compute importance: Extract feature importance as regulatory edge weights
steps.step3: Add sign: Compute Pearson correlation between TF and target expression; sign = sign(correlation)
steps.step4: Train on PBMC: For each target gene, train RF to predict from TF expression only
steps.step5: Extract network: Top-k edges by importance, signed by correlation
steps.step6: Evaluate: Compute AUROC, AUPRC, Signed AUROC against TRRUST/ChIP-seq

title: Implement and Run scMultiomeGRN Baseline
description: Strong baseline: GNN-based multi-omics GRN inference (simplified implementation)

steps.step1: Implement scMultiomeGRN architecture: 2-layer GCN with cross-modal attention
steps.step2: Build graph: Create k-NN graph (k=15) from RNA expression + ATAC accessibility
steps.step3: Train on PBMC: 200 epochs, lr=1e-3, batch_size=256, hidden_dim=256
steps.step4: Extract GRN: Get TF-target edge weights from learned attention
steps.step5: Evaluate on test set: AUROC, AUPRC against ChIP-seq
steps.step6: Run 3 seeds: seeds=[42, 43, 44] for statistical significance
steps.estimated_time: 45 minutes on GPU (15 min per seed)

title: Implement and Run XATGRN-adapted Baseline
description: Adapt XATGRN's cross-attention GNN for single-cell multi-omics comparison

steps.step1: Implement XATGRN architecture: GNN with dual complex graph embedding and cross-attention
steps.step2: Adapt for single-cell: Use cell-level graphs instead of bulk gene-gene graphs
steps.step3: Train on PBMC multi-omics: Same training setup as our method for fair comparison
steps.step4: Extract signed GRN: Use cross-attention weights and sign prediction head
steps.step5: Evaluate: AUROC, AUPRC, sign accuracy
steps.estimated_time: 45 minutes on GPU (15 min per seed)

title: Implement CROSS-GRN Architecture
description: Implement the full CROSS-GRN model with all components

steps.step1: Implement expression encoder: 8-layer transformer, hidden_dim=384, num_heads=6, ff_dim=1024
steps.step2: Implement accessibility encoder: 8-layer transformer, same config as expression encoder
steps.step3: Implement dual asymmetric cross-attention: Separate Q/K projections for TF→target and target→TF
steps.step4: Implement cell-type conditioning: Learnable embeddings (num_cell_types x 128) + MLP modulation
steps.step5: Implement prediction heads: Expression prediction (MSE), edge existence (BCE), edge sign (MSE to tanh)
steps.step7: Verify parameter count: Should be ~35M parameters total

title: Train CROSS-GRN on PBMC Dataset
description: Full training of CROSS-GRN with 3 random seeds for statistical testing

steps.step1: Setup training config: lr=1e-4 with cosine decay, batch_size=64, epochs=100, gradient_clip=1.0
steps.step2: Train seed 42: python train_crossgrn.py --dataset pbmc --seed 42 --output models/crossgrn_pbmc_s42.pt
steps.step3: Train seed 43: python train_crossgrn.py --dataset pbmc --seed 43 --output models/crossgrn_pbmc_s43.pt
steps.step4: Train seed 44: python train_crossgrn.py --dataset pbmc --seed 44 --output models/crossgrn_pbmc_s44.pt
steps.step5: Monitor training: Track loss curves, validation AUROC every 10 epochs
steps.step6: Extract GRNs: For each seed, extract cell-type-specific GRNs from trained model
steps.estimated_time: 2 hours on GPU (40 min per seed)

title: Evaluate CROSS-GRN vs Baselines on PBMC
description: Comprehensive evaluation comparing CROSS-GRN to all baselines

steps.step1: Compute GRN metrics: AUROC, AUPRC, EPR@100 against ChIP-seq for each method
steps.step2: Compute signed metrics: Signed AUROC, sign prediction accuracy on TRRUST
steps.step3: Run statistical tests: Paired t-test between CROSS-GRN and each baseline (p < 0.05)
steps.step4: Test directionality: Compare forward vs reverse attention predictive power (t-test)
steps.step5: Evaluate expression prediction: Pearson r, R² on masked gene prediction task
steps.step6: Compile results table: Mean ± std across 3 seeds for all methods

title: Ablation: Symmetric vs Asymmetric Attention
description: Test the importance of asymmetric cross-attention design

steps.step1: Modify architecture: Replace asymmetric with symmetric cross-attention (shared Q projections)
steps.step2: Train symmetric model: Same hyperparameters, 3 seeds [42, 43, 44]
steps.step3: Compare performance: Full model vs symmetric on AUROC, AUPRC, sign accuracy
steps.step4: Statistical test: Paired t-test on performance difference (p < 0.05)
steps.step5: Analyze attention patterns: Compare forward vs reverse attention weights

title: Ablation: Cell-Type Conditioning
description: Test the importance of cell-type-conditioned attention modulation

steps.step1: Modify architecture: Remove cell-type embeddings and modulation (static model)
steps.step2: Train static model: Same hyperparameters, 3 seeds
steps.step3: Compare performance: Full model vs static on GRN metrics
steps.step4: Evaluate cell-type specificity: Compute Jaccard similarity between GRNs of different cell types
steps.step5: Statistical test: Paired t-test (p < 0.05)

title: Ablation: Sign Prediction and Multi-Omics
description: Test signed edge prediction and multi-omics integration

steps.step1: Ablation without sign: Train model predicting only edge existence, not sign
steps.step2: Ablation RNA-only: Train with expression data only, no ATAC
steps.step3: Train both: 3 seeds each, same hyperparameters
steps.step4: Compare: Full model vs no-sign vs RNA-only on all metrics
steps.step5: Analyze sign accuracy: Correlation between predicted and true signs

title: Cell-Type-Specific GRN Analysis
description: Detailed analysis of cell-type-specific regulatory networks

steps.step1: Extract cell-type GRNs: For each of ~10 cell types, aggregate attention weights
steps.step2: Compute Jaccard similarities: Pairwise Jaccard between cell-type GRNs (top 1000 edges)
steps.step3: Analyze variation: Verify Jaccard 0.3-0.7 between related types, lower for distant types
steps.step4: TF enrichment: Test if cell-type-specific TFs (e.g., CD3E for T cells) have higher degree in respective networks
steps.step5: Identify dynamic edges: Find TF-target pairs with >2x weight difference across cell types
steps.step6: Visualize: Heatmap of Jaccard similarities, top TFs per cell type

title: Generalization: Train and Evaluate on SHARE-seq
description: Test generalization to different tissue (mouse skin)

steps.step1: Train CROSS-GRN on SHARE-seq: 3 seeds, same hyperparameters as PBMC
steps.step2: Train baselines on SHARE-seq: GENIE3, scMultiomeGRN for comparison
steps.step3: Evaluate: AUROC, AUPRC (no ChIP-seq ground truth, use conservation with known motifs)
steps.step4: Compare trends: Verify relative performance matches PBMC results
steps.step5: Save: results/crossgrn_shareseq_results.json

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Rigorous statistical testing of all claims

steps.step1: Main hypothesis test: Paired t-test CROSS-GRN vs scMultiomeGRN AUROC, p < 0.05
steps.step2: Main hypothesis test: Paired t-test CROSS-GRN vs AttentionGRN AUROC, p < 0.05
steps.step3: Directionality test: Paired t-test forward vs reverse attention, p < 0.01
steps.step4: Sign prediction test: Binomial test for sign accuracy vs random (50%), p < 0.001
steps.step5: Ablation tests: Paired t-tests for each ablation, p < 0.05
steps.step6: XATGRN comparison: Paired t-test CROSS-GRN vs XATGRN, p < 0.05
steps.step7: Multiple testing correction: Apply Bonferroni correction where appropriate

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Verify all success criteria from proposal are met

steps.step1: Criterion 1: GRN AUROC > baselines with p < 0.05
steps.step2: Criterion 2: Forward attention > reverse attention with p < 0.01
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Sign accuracy > 70% with p < 0.001 vs random
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Cell-type conditioning improves over static with p < 0.05
steps.step5: Criterion 5: CROSS-GRN > XATGRN on multi-omics data with p < 0.05
steps.step6: Criterion 6: Pearson r > 0.85 on expression prediction
steps.step7: Document: results/success_criteria_check.md with pass/fail for each
steps.step8: If criteria not met: Document failure analysis and potential reasons

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create publication-quality figures for the paper

steps.step1: Figure 1 (Architecture): Draw CROSS-GRN architecture diagram with asymmetric attention
steps.step2: Figure 2 (Main Results): Bar plot comparing AUROC/AUPRC across methods with error bars (3 seeds)
steps.step3: Figure 3 (Ablation): Grouped bar plot showing contribution of each component
steps.step4: Figure 4 (Cell-Type GRNs): Heatmap of Jaccard similarities + network visualization for 2 cell types
steps.step5: Figure 5 (Directionality): Comparison of forward vs reverse attention predictive power
steps.step6: Supplementary: Training curves, sign prediction confusion matrix, attention weight distributions

title: Generate Results Tables
description: Create publication-ready tables with all results

steps.step1: Table 1 (Main Results): Method | AUROC | AUPRC | Sign Acc | Pearson r | with std across seeds
steps.step2: Table 2 (Ablation Study): Component | AUROC | delta vs full | p-value
steps.step3: Table 3 (Cell-Type Analysis): Cell Type | #TFs | #Edges | Top TF | Jaccard range
steps.step4: Table 4 (Statistical Tests): Comparison | Test | Statistic | p-value | Significant?
steps.step5: Save: results/tables.md with LaTeX and markdown versions

title: Generate Final Summary Report
description: Compile all results into comprehensive summary

steps.step1: Summarize main findings: Best AUROC, improvement over baselines
steps.step2: Summarize ablation results: Contribution of each component
steps.step3: Summarize cell-type analysis: Key findings about context-specific GRNs
steps.step4: Address XATGRN differentiation: Explicit comparison showing multi-omics + transformer advantage
steps.step5: Document limitations: Dataset size, missing perturbation data
steps.step6: Generate plan_runtime_summary.md with actual vs estimated times
